title: Ly Cong Uan Ascends the Throne
summary: In 1009, Ly Cong Uan was honored as King by the imperial court, opening the powerful Ly Dynasty and performing a historic move of the capital to Thang Long.

slides.text: Ly Cong Uan was a virtuous and talented man, trusted by the mandarins and honored as King after the death of King Le Long Dinh.
slides.imagePrompt: Ly Cong Uan being invited by mandarins to be the King, respectful ceremony in ancient palace, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Upon ascending the throne, he decided to move the capital from the mountainous region of Hoa Lu to the Dai La Citadel to allow the country to develop further.
slides.imagePrompt: King Ly Cong Uan standing on a boat, traveling on the Red River, looking towards a new land, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When the dragon boat arrived, the King saw a golden dragon rising into the sky and named the new land Thang Long (Rising Dragon).
slides.imagePrompt: A golden dragon rising from the ground into the sky, King Ly Cong Uan and his entourage amazed, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Thang Long thereafter became the brilliant and prosperous capital of Dai Viet for hundreds of years.
slides.imagePrompt: Ancient Thang Long citadel with bustling markets and beautiful temples, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Where did Ly Cong Uan move the capital from Hoa Lu?
When moving the capital, what animal did the King see rising from the new land?
